headline: Samsung and SK Hynix crash 9%+ as chip rout spreads
detail: A major overnight selloff in the world's two largest memory chipmakers signals broad semiconductor sector stress, not just one company's problem. This directly pressures SMH, SOXL, and any chip-adjacent names in your book. On margin, sector ETF drawdowns can accelerate quickly.
rec: Seriously consider trimming SOXL here or tightening the stop closer to current price - the catalyst for more downside just got louder. SMH stop at $540 becomes more relevant today; don't ignore it.

headline: Google loses final appeal on $4.7B EU antitrust fine
detail: Confirms EU is willing and able to impose massive penalties on Big Tech. While Google is the direct target, this keeps regulatory risk elevated across large-cap tech broadly. No immediate impact on your specific chip holdings but adds a negative sentiment overhang for QQQ.
rec: No action needed today - you hold QQQ with a 1.47% gain and a $670 stop that's well below current price. Just be aware this adds a soft headwind to big-tech sentiment.

headline: OpenAI proposes 5% stake to Trump administration
detail: OpenAI trying to ease Washington pressure by offering the government an equity stake is a notable AI governance story. Could be read as AI sector trying to avoid harsh regulation, which is net positive for AI-adjacent chip demand longer term. Indirect positive for names like CRWD and chip suppliers.
rec: No action - this is background noise for your book today. CRWD is your only AI-adjacent holding and it's already up 7.4% with no stop concern. Watch, don't chase.

headline: M&A is 'on fire' as large-cap firms simplify, Citi UK CEO says
detail: A pickup in large-cap M&A activity can be a positive signal for tech valuations and risk appetite broadly. Historically benefits software and cybersecurity names like CRWD as acquisition targets command premiums. Supportive but not a same-day catalyst.
rec: No action today - CRWD is holding well and this is a tailwind story, not a trigger. Keep your stop at $590 and let it run.
